title: California Pedestrian Accident Lawyer | Frank Penney Injury Law
metaDescription: California pedestrian accident lawyer for victims hit by cars and trucks. $1B+ recovered. Free consultation 24/7. No fee unless we win. Home & hospital visits.
heroSubheadline: Hit While Walking? Get a Free Case Review.
whyText: Pedestrians struck by motor vehicles often suffer life-altering injuries. Frank Penney Injury Law has 30+ years of experience holding negligent drivers accountable for hitting walkers, joggers, and pedestrians across California.
citiesTitle: California Pedestrian Accident Lawyers Across Northern California
citiesLede: Frank Penney Injury Law represents pedestrian accident victims across Sacramento, Roseville, Fairfield, Stockton, Modesto, Chico, and the rest of Northern California. From crosswalk crashes to hit-and-run incidents to parking-lot pedestrian strikes, our pedestrian accident attorneys handle every kind of walker injury case. California Vehicle Code §21950 requires drivers to yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks — drivers who violate that duty are liable for the injuries they cause.
serviceDescription: Representing California pedestrian accident victims with $1 Billion+ recovered. We handle crosswalk strikes, hit-and-run cases, and pedestrian injuries throughout Sacramento, Roseville, Fairfield, Stockton, Modesto, and Chico.

faq.q: What rights do pedestrians have in California crosswalks?
faq.a: California Vehicle Code §21950 requires drivers to yield to pedestrians in any marked or unmarked crosswalk. Even outside crosswalks, drivers must exercise due care for pedestrian safety. A driver who hits a pedestrian in a crosswalk is presumed at fault in most cases.

faq.q: What if I was hit while jaywalking?
faq.a: Jaywalking may reduce your compensation under California's pure comparative negligence rule, but it doesn't eliminate your claim. Drivers still owe a duty of care to all pedestrians, even those crossing outside crosswalks. We push hard against insurance companies that try to inflate a pedestrian's percentage of fault.

faq.q: What if the driver fled the scene of the pedestrian accident?
faq.a: Hit-and-run pedestrian cases are common. If you have uninsured/underinsured motorist (UM/UIM) coverage on your auto policy, that coverage typically applies even when you were on foot. We also work to identify hit-and-run drivers via surveillance video, witness statements, and police investigation.

faq.q: How much is a pedestrian accident case worth?
faq.a: Pedestrian injuries are often catastrophic — there's no metal frame between you and the vehicle. Settlements range from tens of thousands for moderate injuries to multi-million-dollar verdicts for serious or permanent injury. Frank Penney Injury Law has recovered $1.2M+ on a single pedestrian case.

faq.q: Who is at fault if a pedestrian is hit in a parking lot?
faq.a: Drivers in parking lots have an even higher duty of care because pedestrians are everywhere. Most parking-lot pedestrian strikes are the driver's fault — though comparative negligence may apply if the pedestrian stepped out from between vehicles unexpectedly. Surveillance video usually decides these cases.

faq.q: How long do I have to file a pedestrian accident lawsuit in California?
faq.a: Generally two years from the date of the accident under California's personal injury statute of limitations. Government-defendant cases (e.g., a city vehicle striking a pedestrian) require a claim within six months.

faq.q: What if a distracted or impaired driver hit me?
faq.a: Distracted driving (texting, phone use) and DUI are leading causes of pedestrian fatalities. Phone records, dashcam footage, and BAC results can prove the other driver's fault and may also support claims for punitive damages. We subpoena these records aggressively.

faq.q: What evidence is critical in a pedestrian injury case?
faq.a: The police report, photos of the scene, surveillance video from nearby businesses, witness statements, the driver's insurance, and your medical records. We dispatch investigators quickly to preserve evidence — surveillance video is often overwritten within days.

faq.q: Can I recover for emotional trauma after a pedestrian accident?
faq.a: Yes. Pedestrian crash victims often suffer significant emotional and psychological trauma in addition to physical injuries. California law allows recovery for emotional distress, pain and suffering, and loss of enjoyment of life. We work with mental health professionals to fully document the impact.

faq.q: How much does it cost to hire a pedestrian accident lawyer?
faq.a: Frank Penney Injury Law works on a contingency basis. You pay nothing up front and no fee unless we win. Free consultations are available 24/7 by phone at (888) 888-0566 or in person at any of our 6 California offices. We also make home and hospital visits.

testimonials.author: Jennifer M.
testimonials.text: The settlement was more than I could have received on my own and the fact that I could hand it all over to him to resolve was without a doubt worth it! It was the best decision I ever made throughout this process.

testimonials.author: Robert S.
testimonials.text: At a time when I was most vulnerable, I received support and understanding. Frank Penney Injury Lawyers provide excellent customer service. I cannot recommend them enough to my friends and family!

testimonials.author: Maria L.
testimonials.text: I would highly recommend this firm for those that do not know anything about the procedures that take place after a car injury. They were responsive, accommodating, and I had a good feeling about the firm from the start.
